title: 30-Year-Old Tech Lead: Balancing High Stress & FIRE Goals
insurance_needs:
【客户案例分析】
The client, David, is a Senior Backend Engineer. While earning a high salary, he faces the '996' work culture (9am-9pm, 6 days a week), severe sleep deprivation, and a sedentary lifestyle. 

1. **Critical Illness Protection**: Tech professionals have a statistically higher risk of sudden cardiac arrest and early-onset cancer. The client needs a multi-claim CI policy that covers 'Pre-illness' conditions.
2. **Medical Gap**: His company provides group medical, but it has sub-limits. He needs a 'Full Cover' private hospital plan (VHIS) to avoid public hospital queues.
3. **Liability**: As the sole breadwinner for his parents, he needs a safety net.
4. **Liquidity**: He prefers flexible cash flow but wants forced savings for early retirement.
recommended_products: AIA 友邦 - On Your Side Plan (自在自選危疾保) - [Critical Illness]
AXA 安盛 - WiseGuard Pro Medical Insurance Plan (智尊守衛) - [High-end VHIS]
Prudential 保誠 - Wealth Flow (雋富多元貨幣計劃) - [Savings/Wealth]
case_description:
David is typical of Hong Kong's tech elite: cash-rich but time-poor, with significant health anxieties. We structured his portfolio to protect his earning power first, then grow his wealth.

【财富管理规划】
Since David aims to retire by age 45 (FIRE), we allocated approximately 15% of his annual income into **Prudential's Wealth Flow**. This plan allows him to diversify his HKD salary into a Global/USD asset basket. By paying a premium for 5 years, the compound interest effect will project a cash value capable of replacing his salary by the 15th-20th policy year. This creates a 'passive income generator' that functions regardless of his employment status in the volatile tech sector.
key_points: Prioritized 'Benign Tumor' coverage in CI plan due to high-stress lifestyle
Utilized VHIS deductible option to lower medical premiums by 40%
Short-term payment term (5 years) for savings to align with high-income window
budget_suggestion: Approx. 15-18% of Annual Income (Protection: 3% / Wealth Accumulation: 12-15%)
